description: Black News Digest, published by the U.S. Department of Labor Office of Information and Public Affairs, aimed to provide a comprehensive overview of current events, news, and analysis related to the African American community. The periodical, first published in 1971, sought to address the information needs of readers interested in issues and happenings of relevance to the Black community. Presented in a news digest format, it delivered concise summaries of articles from various sources, offering a wide range of perspectives on topics such as civil rights, social justice, education, employment, and culture. Black News Digest covered a broad array of subjects, reflecting the diversity of experiences and concerns within the African American community. The publication featured stories on politics, activism, community development, arts, and culture. In addition to news articles, it included opinion pieces, interviews, and editorials, providing readers with a comprehensive view of current events and discourses shaping the lives of African Americans. Black News Digest is no longer being published, as it ceased publication sometime in 199?. Despite its discontinuation, the periodical remains an important resource for researchers interested in understanding the historical context and perspectives on issues relevant to the Black community during its publication period.
subject: Business & Economics
title: Black News Digest
